rationale: Y independently surfaces the async_chunk default-asymmetry (matching the GT Low finding almost exactly, with a concrete fix recommendation) and separately flags that the split now keys solely on total_num_scheduled_tokens, noting main later generalized to_payload_element to also match hidden-shape alignment — this precisely lands on the latent multi-axis gap later fixed by PR #4910, earning gap_hit. X's four findings are grounded but are lower-value style/documentation nits (add a comment, extract a helper, add an assertion) that never engage with the multi-axis splitting risk, so it covers fewer live reviewer concerns and misses the gap entirely.
